name: Colonial Goose
recipeInstructions:
1. FOR STUFFING:
- 4 oz Dried apricots
- 4 oz Fresh white breadcrumbs
- 1 oz Butter
- 1 tbsp Clear honey
- 2 oz Onion, chopped
- 1/4 tsp Dried thyme
- 1/4 tsp Salt
- Pepper
- 1 medium Egg, beaten
2. FOR MARINADE:
- 1/2 lb Old carrots, sliced
- 6 oz Onions, sliced
- 1 Bayleaf
- 3 Parsley stalks, crushed
- 5 fl Red wine
Overnight Before: Make the stuffing by chopping apricots coarsely and mixing with breadcrumbs, chopped onions. Melt butter, pour over, add honey, salt, pepper, thyme. Mix well, then mix in beaten egg.
Overnight Before: Make marinade by mixing all ingredients together. Bone or have boned the lamb. Stuff cavity loosely. Seal opening. Place lamb in a polythene bag long enough to hold it comfortably. Add bones. Pour over marinade, tie top with bag seal, sucking out excess air. Stand in a bowl (to catch leaks). Marinade overnight, turning from time to time.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F (Mk 4). Remove lamb from marinade and dry well. Drain marinade and fry vegetables briefly in roasting pan. Place lamb on top and roast for 25 minutes per pound of oven-ready weight. Baste frequently with marinade while cooking. Meanwhile, make a little lamb stock from bones.
4. When done, remove from oven. Remove strings from joint and let rest 15 minutes while making gravy. For the gravy, remove vegetables from roasting tin, pour off roasting juices (leaving 2 tbsp fat in pan), fry 2 tbsp flour in this, return aqueous part of roasting juices and remaining marinade. Add lamb stock if needed. Season to taste.
